product.subject: fitness coaching and class booking
product.primaryJob: discover, compare, and book the right fitness service

direction.thesis: A dark training interface that feels like calibrated sports equipment rather than a generic gaming dashboard.
direction.metaphor: measured physical cadence and training instruments
direction.signatureElement: A progress rail that behaves like an interval-training timeline and connects discovery, booking, and attendance.
forbiddenPatterns: all content inside identical rounded cards

dials.notes: Distinctive composition and one strong motion sequence; normal task interactions remain restrained.

input.value: dark red and black sporty theme

typography.display: A condensed or athletic display face with full required language support
typography.body: A highly readable UI family with Persian and Latin compatibility
typography.utility: A tabular-number face for time, distance, price, and progress data

layout.mobile: task-first bottom navigation and edge-to-edge primary flow
layout.tablet: split discovery and detail when space permits
layout.desktop: expanded application shell, not a stretched phone frame

motion.mainSequence: interval-like progress movement during onboarding and booking confirmation
motion.functionalFeedback: short state transitions for filters, selections, and validation
motion.reducedMotion: instant state changes with opacity-only feedback

databaseInputs: used only to compare fitness UX patterns and accessibility risks

research.evidenceStatus: proposal and user-stated direction only; no validated end-user research

claims.claim: mobile booking should be the primary journey
new members value fast service comparison

validationNeeded: compare booking-first and progress-first home-screen models with target users

content.voice: direct, active, supportive without motivational filler
stressTests: mixed LTR prices, phone numbers, and URLs
